g.data-narration: Welcome to SQL Data Definition and Data Types - the foundation of database management systems.
g.text: SQL Data Definition Language & Data Types

g.data-narration: DDL commands define database structure. CREATE makes objects, ALTER modifies them, and DROP removes them entirely.
g.text: Data Definition Language (DDL) commands manage database structures:

g.data-narration: Proper data type selection ensures data integrity, optimizes storage, and enables efficient query execution in databases.
g.text: Choosing correct data types provides three key benefits:
Wrong type → data corruption, wasted storage, slow queries

g.data-narration: Numeric types include INTEGER for whole numbers, DECIMAL for exact precision, and FLOAT for approximate scientific values.
CHAR pads strings to fixed length while VARCHAR stores variable-length text efficiently. TEXT handles large text content.
DATE stores calendar dates, TIME stores time, and DATETIME combines both for complete timestamp information.
BOOLEAN stores true/false, BLOB stores binary data, and NULL represents missing values - not zero or empty string.
g.text: Use IS NULL / IS NOT NULL

g.data-narration: CREATE TABLE defines a new table with columns, data types, and optional constraints for data validation rules.
g.text: Syntax for creating a new table:

g.data-narration: This example shows a students table with PRIMARY KEY, NOT NULL, UNIQUE, and CHECK constraints for data integrity.
ALTER TABLE modifies existing tables by adding, modifying, or dropping columns and constraints without losing data.
g.text: ALTER TABLE t ADD col TYPE;
ALTER TABLE t MODIFY c TYPE;
ALTER TABLE t DROP COLUMN c;

g.data-narration: DROP removes the entire table structure, TRUNCATE deletes all rows but keeps structure, DELETE removes rows conditionally.
PRIMARY KEY uniquely identifies rows (one per table, not null). UNIQUE ensures no duplicates but allows null values.
g.text: ✓ One per table ✓ NOT NULL ✓ Unique
*Database-dependent: some databases allow multiple NULL values in UNIQUE columns

g.data-narration: FOREIGN KEY creates relationships between tables, enforcing referential integrity so child records reference valid parent records.
g.text: ON DELETE actions: CASCADE (delete child) | RESTRICT (prevent delete) | SET NULL

g.data-narration: NOT NULL forces value presence, DEFAULT provides fallback values, and CHECK validates data against specified conditions.
g.text: Column must always have a value
Value when none provided: DEFAULT CURRENT_DATE

g.data-narration: CHAR pads to fixed length, VARCHAR saves storage. NULL is not zero. PRIMARY KEY combines UNIQUE plus NOT NULL.
Choose INT for IDs, DECIMAL for money, VARCHAR for text. Practice CREATE TABLE and ALTER operations for exams.

g.text: INT for identifiers, DECIMAL for monetary values
PRIMARY KEY = UNIQUE + NOT NULL combined
FOREIGN KEY data type must match referenced PK
ALTER preserves existing data when modifying
Use CASCADE carefully - can delete related data

g.data-narration: Congratulations! You have completed the SQL DDL and Data Types module. Practice these concepts to master database design.
g.text: Module Complete - Practice creating tables and applying constraints to reinforce learning
